summary: sync-grove postinstall fetches and executes attacker-controlled command over plain HTTP
details: Package.json declares `"postinstall": "node setup.js"`, so `setup.js` runs automatically on `npm install`. The script assembles the strings `shelljs` and `exec` from base64 fragments at runtime, then fetches two plain-HTTP URLs on player.sweeprovider.org (`/getKey.php` and `/generateRandomKey.php`) whose values are stored as concatenated base64 fragments (`securityKey1`), decrypts the response with a hardcoded AES salt, and passes the resulting string to shelljs.exec on the installer's host. The package presents itself as a typosquat of `sync-exec` ("Synchronous exec with status code support"), and `js/syncgrove.js` mirrors the sync-exec implementation as a decoy. The combination of hardcoded remote HTTP endpoints, runtime-assembled module/method names, encrypted payload staging, and shell execution at install time is a supply-chain dropper: whatever command the attacker serves runs with the installer's privileges on `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
